_comment: Pipeline Doctor - preventive Splunk alert templates. Each rule maps 1:1 to one fault scenario, because each scenario breaks exactly one DQ dimension (null_rate / row_count / freshness_lag_min) on the inventory chain. Thresholds mirror generate_data.py (null_rate>0.05, row_count<base*0.7, freshness_lag_min>20). Edit `search`, `cron_schedule`, or `severity` here; generate_alerts.py pushes whatever is in this file.

alerts.name: PipelineDoctor - Inventory null_rate breach (schema_change)
alerts.description: Fires when a null_rate DQ check fails on the inventory chain. Catches an un-propagated source column rename (e.g. stock_count -> available_qty) before the dashboard goes wrong.
alerts.search: index=main sourcetype=pipeline:data_quality check_name=null_rate status=fail (table="raw.inventory_snapshot" OR table="mart.product_availability") | stats max(value) as max_null_rate latest(threshold) as threshold values(column) as affected_columns by table

alerts.name: PipelineDoctor - Inventory row_count collapse (volume_drop)
alerts.description: Fires when a row_count DQ check fails on the inventory chain. Catches a partial source extraction where the ingest job still reports success (WARN, not fail).
alerts.search: index=main sourcetype=pipeline:data_quality check_name=row_count status=fail (table="raw.inventory_snapshot" OR table="mart.product_availability") | stats latest(value) as row_count latest(threshold) as min_expected_rows by table

alerts.name: PipelineDoctor - Inventory freshness SLA breach (freshness_delay)
alerts.description: Fires when a freshness_lag_min DQ check fails on the inventory chain. Catches a stalled source query so you are alerted before business users notice a stale dashboard.
alerts.search: index=main sourcetype=pipeline:data_quality check_name=freshness_lag_min status=fail (table="raw.inventory_snapshot" OR table="mart.product_availability") | stats max(value) as max_lag_min latest(threshold) as threshold_min by table
